transport_2d.reference: src/operators/transport_2d.jl `transport_2d` (imperative reference) — first-order Rusanov / local Lax-Friedrichs flux-form 2D transport on the gnomonic cubed sphere with C-grid staggered Courant numbers. Equivalent to first-order donor-cell upwind when the LF dissipation coefficient is set to the local face Courant magnitude; retained for symbolic / debugging use as the simplest cubed-sphere transport scheme (cf. catalog row `lax_friedrichs_flux`). Higher-order Lin-Rood and unsplit-PPM transport on the same grid live in transport_2d.jl alongside this rule and remain pending separate beads (catalog rows `fv3_lin_rood_advection`, `cam5_fv_ppm_reconstruction`).
transport_2d.notes: Flux-form first-order FV transport: dq/dt = -(1/A)·(F_E·dx_E - F_W·dx_W + G_N·dy_N - G_S·dy_S), with each face flux F = c·(q_L + q_R)/2 - |c|·(q_R - q_L)/2 (local Lax-Friedrichs, dissipation coefficient = |c|). Expanding the symmetric and dissipative parts gives a 5-point in-panel stencil over offsets (0,0), (±1,0), (0,±1) with coefficients that depend on per-face Courant numbers `c_xi_E`, `c_xi_W`, `c_eta_N`, `c_eta_S` and the corresponding face-length / cell-area metrics bound at the central cell by the cubed_sphere accessor. Cross-panel ghost cells and basis rotation at panel boundaries are handled below the rule layer (src/grids/panel_connectivity.jl); selectors carry only in-panel `(xi, eta)` offsets and do NOT carry a `panel` field. Same convention as `covariant_laplacian_cubed_sphere` for the metric/accessor split.
transport_2d.panel_handling: lives in the cubed_sphere grid accessor (src/grids/panel_connectivity.jl); the rule references in-panel (xi, eta) offsets only. Selectors do NOT carry a `panel` field — cross-panel ghost extension and basis rotation happen below the rule layer.

metric_bindings.comment: Per-cell bindings resolved by the cubed_sphere grid accessor at the central stencil cell (offset 0,0). Face-staggered Courant numbers and face lengths are accessor-resolved from the face arrays at the four faces of the central cell. Names follow the snake_case convention of src/grids/cubed_sphere.jl and src/operators/transport_2d.jl.
metric_bindings.c_xi_E: Courant number at the east (xi+1/2) face of the central cell
metric_bindings.c_xi_W: Courant number at the west (xi-1/2) face of the central cell
metric_bindings.c_eta_N: Courant number at the north (eta+1/2) face of the central cell
metric_bindings.c_eta_S: Courant number at the south (eta-1/2) face of the central cell
metric_bindings.dx_E: physical edge length of the east face (grid.dx[p, i+1, j])
metric_bindings.dx_W: physical edge length of the west face (grid.dx[p, i, j])
metric_bindings.dy_N: physical edge length of the north face (grid.dy[p, i, j+1])
metric_bindings.dy_S: physical edge length of the south face (grid.dy[p, i, j])

stencil.comment: Center cell (0,0): self-coefficient from the inflow side of every face. East face contributes -((c_E + |c_E|)/2)·dx_E/A, west face contributes +((c_W - |c_W|)/2)·dx_W/A, and similarly for the eta faces.
East neighbor (+1, 0): outflow-from-east contribution at the east face only. Coefficient = -((c_E - |c_E|)/2)·dx_E/A; nonzero only when c_E < 0.
West neighbor (-1, 0): inflow-from-west contribution at the west face only. Coefficient = +((c_W + |c_W|)/2)·dx_W/A; nonzero only when c_W > 0.
North neighbor (0, +1): outflow-from-north contribution at the north face only. Coefficient = -((c_N - |c_N|)/2)·dy_N/A; nonzero only when c_N < 0.
South neighbor (0, -1): inflow-from-south contribution at the south face only. Coefficient = +((c_S + |c_S|)/2)·dy_S/A; nonzero only when c_S > 0.
